transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Let's get started. Today is the last Friday of Sprint 3 and we're kicking off Sprint 4 planning. I want a clean wrap-up of Sprint 3 first, then we talk about what's ahead. Rupali, take it away.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Thank you sir. Sprint 3 wrap-up — auth module is fully delivered and deployed. Client confirmed sign-off on Wednesday. Test coverage was 91% across the auth service, which is actually above our target of 85%. So that's a genuine win. On the not-so-good side — staging environment parity was an issue again this sprint. We had 3 instances where staging config didn't match prod expectations and that caused test rework.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: I want to add to that. The test rework on staging parity alone cost us about 2 days across the sprint. And this is the second sprint in a row we've had this. Auth module would have been delivered even earlier without that drag.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Agreed. Sprint 3 technically went well on the development side. The auth module — OIDC integration, token refresh logic, session management — all clean. The team delivered quality work. Staging parity is an ops issue that keeps recurring.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: I want to be transparent here. Some of the staging config drift happened because central infra team made changes to prod without communicating to us. I can maintain our side but I can't control what they do on their end without being in those conversations.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: We'll come back to the process fix. For now let's talk Sprint 4. Client confirmed this morning — go-live date is April 9. That is fixed. Payment API, end to end. Rupali — what's the Sprint 4 scope as we've agreed?

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Sprint 4 scope — payment API integration with client's gateway, full payment flow frontend, complete QA coverage including edge cases, and production deployment. We start Monday April 6. Go-live is April 9 — that's Thursday of Week 1. So we have Monday to Wednesday to build, test and deploy. It's aggressive.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Aggressive is the right word. I want to flag something right now before Monday. The payment gateway integration needs the sandbox credentials and API documentation from client. We've been asking since last Monday. Varsha, do we have them yet?

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: Credentials arrived Tuesday but the API doc is still incomplete. The response schema for payment initiation is missing. Client's technical team said they'll send it by today EOD. I haven't received it yet.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: If we don't have that by Monday morning, we can't start integration properly. Rupali can you follow up directly with the client contact before 5pm today?

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: I'm on it, I'll send a message after this call. Vinod sir — client confirmed April 9 verbally this morning? Do we have it in writing?

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Yes, email at 9:15am. They've communicated it internally to their payments team and operations team. Their people are expecting the go-live. So there's real downstream pressure on them too.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: Can I raise the infra dependencies? I need to know before Monday. First — staging environment needs the DB config updated to point to Sprint 4 payment tables. That's my ticket, I can do it today or over the weekend. But second — and this is the bigger one — I need confirmation from central infra team that the prod network security group allows outbound calls to the payment gateway IP range. That's not in our control.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: That's a good catch Vivek. Can you raise that ticket to central infra today itself? What's the ETA if you raise it now?

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: Normally they take 24-48 hours. If I raise now, realistically I'd hear back Monday. Which means we don't know by Monday morning if the network will allow those calls.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Raise it now Vivek. Include Vinod sir's name in the ticket as the escalation point. That usually speeds it up.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: Okay, raising it right now. One other thing — the DB config update for staging. I'll do that today so it's ready Monday morning.

transcript.speaker: Suraj
transcript.text: From my side — I have the payment UI wireframes and I've started the component structure. I need the API response schema to finalize the confirmation page field mapping. So Varsha's point about the API doc applies to me too.

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: I'll share whatever I have as soon as it comes in. If the full doc isn't available by Monday I'll write a best-guess schema based on the credentials we have, and we can correct it when the real doc arrives.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: That works. Better to start with assumptions and correct than to wait and lose a day.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Okay. To summarise — Sprint 3 closed well, auth module delivered. Sprint 4 starts Monday, go-live April 9 is confirmed. Outstanding items before Monday: Rupali follows up on API doc, Vivek raises the infra network ticket and updates DB config today, Varsha preps a working schema if doc doesn't arrive. Any concerns I haven't heard?

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: I'd like the full test plan reviewed before Monday. I have 34 test cases drafted. Can Rakesh and Varsha give it a 30-minute review this afternoon? I want to make sure I'm not missing any backend edge cases.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Yes. Let's do it at 3pm today. Varsha, Ajay and I. 30 minutes.

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: I'll be there.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Good. Have a productive weekend. Let's start Sprint 4 on the front foot Monday.
